title: Add periodic cleanup task for expired offline_messages
description:
relay/src/store.rs: drain() only removes messages for a user when they reconnect.

If a user never reconnects, their expired messages accumulate indefinitely:
  - User X authenticates, receives 100 queued DMs
  - After 72h those messages expire
  - User X never reconnects
  - Stale rows persist in database forever

Over a year with 100 concurrent users: ~3.65M stale rows accumulate.

Current design rationale per CLAUDE.md §10: drain-then-deliver is acceptable Phase 1.
But maintenance cleanup is separate from delivery semantics.

Fix options:
  1. DELETE expired messages on relay startup (simplest, Phase 1 appropriate)
  2. Add periodic background task (more robust, scheduled cleanup)
  3. VACUUM ANALYZE after large deletions (optimize query plans)

Start with option 1: Add to Store::new() after all CREATE TABLE statements:
  sqlx::query("DELETE FROM offline_messages WHERE expires_at <= datetime('now')")
    .execute(&pool)
    .await?;

This prevents stale accumulation while keeping Phase 1 one-shot semantics intact.

Cleanup task, not correctness issue. Mark as P3.
